purpose: Replace the slow B-only path with an A-first route plan that also requires calendar display, day-page display, automatic next-fetch route construction, parser integration, and CI validation before JRA is considered complete.

context.current_jra_state: one meeting snapshot plus page-level additional calendar rows; wider section-level records not complete
context.problem_fixed_by_this_plan: Previous schedule checked B/B+ acquisition before requiring display and automatic acquisition, and would have forced a later separate A check.
context.new_policy: Check A first. If A is not possible, downgrade using the same evidence to B+ or B. Do not mark JRA complete until records are normalized, displayed in calendar/day views, and reproducible by an acquisition route.

requires: official race detail or racecard route per race
stable route parameters for future dates
normalized data displayed in calendar and day pages
automatic acquisition route with parser and CI
complete official race_number and race_time rows
normalized data displayed in calendar and day pages
automatic acquisition route with parser and CI
official or source-derived race_count from complete verified rows
fixture/date only or incomplete time coverage

acquisition_verification.description: Official sources must expose enough data to decide A, B+, B, or C from the same route inspection.

normalization.description: Page rows must be split into official meeting sections and normalized into meeting/race/source records.

compressed_forward_schedule.topic: JRA A-first inspection and section-split schema

must_include: split 中山/京都 sections into separate meetings

compressed_forward_schedule.topic: JRA calendar and day-page display adapter

policy_changes: Do not complete JRA at acquisition-result stage only.
Do not run a separate late A-rank investigation after finishing B/B+; inspect A first and downgrade from the same evidence.
Do not promote records that are not shown in calendar and day-page UI.
Do not promote records without a repeatable next-fetch route.
Do not promote records without CI guarding section splits, source URLs, and rank requirements.
